content.text:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

western

Related words are:

- eastern
- cowboy
- movie

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

content.thinking:
I need to describe "western" without using:
- western
- eastern
- cowboy
- movie

I should think about what "western" means:
1. A direction (west)
2. A genre of films/books set in American frontier
3. Related to the west or occidental culture

I'll go with a quick, clear clue focusing on the direction and genre aspects.

CLUE: Direction where the sun sets, or a frontier genre with gunslingers and saloons

content.text: CLUE: Direction where the sun sets; also a film genre featuring gunslingers and saloons in the American frontier

clem_player.response: CLUE: Direction where the sun sets; also a film genre featuring gunslingers and saloons in the American frontier

content.text:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: Direction where the sun sets; also a film genre featuring gunslingers and saloons in the American frontier

content.thinking:
The clue has two parts:
1. "Direction where the sun sets" - this refers to WEST
2. "a film genre featuring gunslingers and saloons in the American frontier" - this is the WESTERN genre

The word that fits both descriptions is "WEST" or "WESTERN". Since it mentions it's a "film genre", the complete answer is likely "WESTERN" which includes "WEST" (the direction where the sun sets) and is also the name of the film genre.

Let me think... the target word is most likely "WESTERN" as it encompasses both meanings perfectly.
